claim: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Completion Reviewer 10 completed a read-only data architecture/performance review across dinobrain and dinobrain-data. Conclusion: AGREE with the intended index-first, SQLite-sharded, graph-aware, provenance-aware direction, but DISAGREE that the current system is scalability-complete. Current live data has operations.sqlite tracked/present, but no wiki.sqlite, no SQLite manifest, no dense-vectors index, and the persisted graph-health is stale/false healthy relative to the accepted corpus. Synthetic verifiers pass but report timings instead of enforcing latency budgets. Completion should require fresh wiki+operations SQLite shards, bounded indexed candidate selection, real BM25/dense retrieval constraints, graph adjacency freshness/health gates, cold-data archive/compaction policy, and explicit latency/freshness gates that fail when the prompt path regresses toward full-vault scans.
